title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction · Behavior Brief: Dom State Render
summary: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js dom state render increment in an accessible interactive service-status interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
